headline: Asian stocks drop again as rollercoaster week draws to close
summary: Asian tech stocks sank again Friday as the rollercoaster ride that has characterised the week continued into the weekend, while crude prices edged back down after a brief rally sparked by news of an attack on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.Oil prices resumed the downward shift that has marked the week, having risen around two percent Thursday on news that a cargo ship was damaged by an unknown projectile off Oman's coast in Hormuz.

content.title: Asian stocks drop again as rollercoaster week draws to close

headline: Apple hikes prices as memory chip costs skyrocket
summary:
STORY: :: Apple

Apple is hiking prices as the cost of memory chips skyrockets.

Its lowest priced laptop will jump from $599 to $699, with iPad prices also rising.

For now at least, the iPhone is unaffected.

Apple says it can no longer avoid passing on rising costs to consumers.

The price of memory chips jumped by around 98% in the first quarter, and has continued to soar ever since.

That is driven by the boom in AI data centers.

Companies like Nvidia have signed big deals with chipmakers, leaving consumer electronics firms scrambling to secure supplies.

Apple said it had never seen component prices increase so much, so quickly.

While iPhones weren’t hit by Thursday’s move, analysts say it’s only a matter of time before they too face price increases.

Apple shares fell almost 5%, while Dell dropped 8%.

Some industry watchers say Apple’s rivals may have to do even bigger price increases, as they lack the Mac maker’s deep ties to suppliers.

Research firm IDC says the global smartphone and computer markets will both see sharp contractions this year due to the rising prices.

While iPhones weren’t hit by Thursday’s move, analysts say it’s only a matter of time before they too face price increases.

Research firm IDC says the global smartphone and computer markets will both see sharp contractions this year due to the rising prices.

headline: Apple (AAPL) Faces £3 Billion UK iCloud Lawsuit As Class Action Moves Ahead
summary: Britain's Competition Appeal Tribunal has allowed a £3b collective lawsuit against Apple to proceed. The case alleges Apple abused its market position in iCloud storage by locking users into its ecosystem. Millions of UK iPhone and iPad users may be eligible to join the class action over alleged overcharging for iCloud subscriptions. Apple, traded as NasdaqGS:AAPL, now faces a large UK class action that directly targets its services business, an important revenue stream alongside hardware...
